title: Software Engineer
description:
GWS Billing Tech Support Agent
   

  

 The GWS Billing QC Support Agent provides high\\-tier support for Google Workspace financial
   

  

 inquiries, ensuring that every resolution is technically sound and follows strict Quality Control
   

  

 (QC) standards. You will handle sensitive tasks such as subscription transitions, refund audits,
   

  

 and account rehabilitations. As a QC\\-focused agent, you are responsible for maintaining the
   

  

 integrity of the billing ecosystem through meticulous self\\-auditing and process adherence.
   

  

 Key ResponsibilitiesSubscription Management Troubleshoot and resolve issues related to GWS license
   

  

 counts, plan upgrades/downgrades (e.g., Business Starter to Business Plus), and
   

  

 annual vs. flexible payment plans.
   

  

* Forensic Billing Analysis Conduct deep\\-dive audits of customer invoices to explain


 "unexplained" charges, often involving proration, tax changes, or domain\\-alias costs.
   

  

* Quality Assurance (QA) Participation Perform peer reviews on billing cases to


 ensure teammates are following standard operating procedures (SOPs) and
   

  

 maintaining "The Google Way" of customer empathy.
   

  

* Payment Triage Resolve failures in auto\\-pay, manual payment processing, and bank


 verification (primary/secondary payment methods).
   

  

* Account Recovery Assist administrators in regaining access to billing consoles in


 cases of lost credentials or organizational changes, ensuring strict identity verification
   

  

 to prevent fraud.
   

  

* Feedback Loop Document "Quality Gaps" or recurring billing bugs and report them


 to the GWS QC Support Team Trainer to improve the overall team’s knowledge base.
   

  

**Technical Skills \\& Qualifications**
* Workspace Admin Knowledge Deep familiarity with the Google Admin Console,


 specifically the "Billing" and "Subscriptions" sections.
   

  

* Financial Literacy Strong understanding of proration, VAT/Sales Tax application,


 and credit/debit memo processing.
   

  

* Platform Proficiency Experience with GWS\\-specific billing tools (e.g., Google Play


 Console for some Workspace apps or the direct GWS Billing interface).

title: Senior Software Engineer
description:
Here at Appian, our values of
 **Intensity** 
 and
 **Excellence** 
 define who we are. We set high standards and live up to them, ensuring that everything we do is done with care and quality. We approach every challenge with ambition and commitment, holding ourselves and each other accountable to achieve the best results. When you join Appian, you’ll be part of a passionate team dedicated to accomplishing hard things, together.
 



  
 



 As a
 **Senior Software Engineer** 
 working on the Appian platform, your mission will be to ensure Appian is always fast, scalable and up to whatever tasks our customers configure it to do. You will be solving problems of scale and flexibility that most engineers never see, building a product capable of serving our customers in ways you never imagined. Your scope extends from the high\\-performance data layer to the cross\\-platform user interface to the design tools used to create it.  
 



**Basic Qualifications (Must have):** 



* 5\\+ years experience building and delivering enterprise\\-grade software applications
* Leverage knowledge of data structures, algorithms, and design patterns to write software in a Java \\& web technology environment.
* Experience in CI/CD in Cloud environments, like AWS and continuous integration systems, like Jenkins
* Respond to and resolve emergent problems; write software and build automation to prevent problem recurrence
* Utilize both object\\-oriented and functional programming approaches in different technologies to implement features effectively.
* Manage availability, latency, scalability, and efficiency of the product by designing reliability into software and systems
* Be a strong contributor to team feature breakdowns/sizing and design of new feature implementations
* Have a high degree of personal responsibility for the overall performance of the team, including capabilities, quality, stability, and velocity
* Seek to identify opportunities for code refactoring and performance optimizations regularly and champion these efforts within the team
* Effectively communicate technical designs verbally and/or in writing for small to medium\\-sized technical audiences.



  
 



**Educational Qualifications:** 



* BE/B.Tech/BS in Computer Science or related technical discipline
* MS in Computer Science preferred
* Demonstrable understanding of systems, algorithms, and OO design or webapi design.



  
 



 This role is based at our office at WTC 11th floor, Old Mahabalipuram Road, SH 49A, Kandhanchavadi, Kottivakkam, Chennai, Tamil Nadu 600041, India. Appian was built on a culture of in\\-person collaboration, which we believe is a key driver of our mission to be the best. Employees hired for this position are expected to be in the office 5 days a week to foster that culture and ensure we continue to thrive through shared ideas and teamwork. We believe being in the office provides more opportunities to come together and celebrate working with the exceptional people across Appian.
 




**Tools and Resources** 




* Training and Development: During onboarding, we focus on equipping new hires with the skills and knowledge for success through department\\-specific training. Continuous learning is a central focus at Appian, with dedicated mentorship and the First\\-Friend program being widely utilized resources for new hires.
* Growth Opportunities: Appian provides a diverse array of growth and development opportunities, including our leadership program tailored for new and aspiring managers, a comprehensive library of specialized department training through Appian University, skills based training, and tuition reimbursement for those aiming to advance their education. This commitment ensures that employees have access to a holistic range of development opportunities.
* Community: We’ll immerse you into our community rooted in respect starting on day one. Appian fosters inclusivity through our 8 employee\\-led affinity groups. These groups help employees build stronger internal and external networks by planning social, educational, and outreach activities to connect with Appianites and larger initiatives throughout the company.



**Benefits**




 Appian offers a comprehensive benefits package designed to support your health, wellbeing, and financial future. Benefits may include health coverage, Employee Assistance Program (EAP) with free mental health support, life and disability insurance, an Employee Stock Purchase Program (ESPP), a retirement/pension plan, wellness dollars, tuition reimbursement, family\\-forming benefits and more. Benefits vary by country—please ask your Talent Acquisition contact for details specific to the location you are applying to.
 




**About Appian** 





 Appian provides process automation technology. We automate complex processes in large enterprises and governments. Our platform is known for its unique reliability and scale. We’ve been automating processes for 25 years and understand enterprise operations like no one else. For more information, visit appian.com. \\[Nasdaq: APPN]
 



 Follow Appian: LinkedIn, Youtube, Instagram, Facebook

title: Associate Software Engineer
description:
**The Role** 
 As an Associate Software Engineer, you will play a critical role in developing and stabilizing a state\\-of\\-the\\-art, cloud\\-native platform. Working alongside Engineering Head, you will apply your mid\\-to\\-senior level experience to navigate modern enterprise architecture, including Micro\\-Frontends, Event\\-Driven microservices, and multi\\-database routing. This is a high\\-impact role requiring deep technical expertise and the ability to mentor junior peers.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities** 




 •
 **Full\\-Stack Development:** 
 Architect and develop complex features across both the frontend (Angular) and backend (Java/Spring Boot).
 



 •
 **Component Architecture:** 
 Design and build reusable, highly optimized UI components within our Angular Micro\\-Frontend (MFE) applications.
 



 •
 **API Engineering:** 
 Write, test, and optimize clean, well\\-documented RESTful APIs using Spring Boot, ensuring high performance across multi\\-database environments.
 



 •
 **Testing \\& Quality Assurance:** 
 Enforce strict TDD practices by writing comprehensive unit and integration tests to ensure code reliability within our CI/CD pipelines.
 



 •
 **System Optimization:** 
 Troubleshoot, debug, and resolve high\\-level architectural issues and performance bottlenecks reported in the issue tracker.
 



 •
 **Technical Leadership:** 
 Actively participate in architectural discussions, code reviews, and provide mentorship to Junior engineers to elevate the team's overall coding standards.
 



**Required Qualifications** 




 • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field.
 



 • 4 to 7 years of proven commercial experience as a Software Engineer.
 



 • Deep fundamental understanding of
 **Java** 
 and Object\\-Oriented Programming (OOP), with extensive experience in
 **Spring Boot** 
 .
 



 • Advanced proficiency in
 **JavaScript/TypeScript** 
 and modern web fundamentals, with strong hands\\-on experience in
 **Angular** 
 .
 



 • Strong understanding of relational databases, complex SQL queries, and ORM tools (Hibernate/JPA). • Advanced familiarity with version control systems (Git) and collaborative CI/ CD platforms (GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket).
 



 • Strong problem\\-solving skills and the ability to work independently on complex architectural challenges. 
 



  





**Nice\\-to\\-Haves** 
 • Experience with cloud\\-native deployment tools (Docker, Kubernetes). • Deep understanding of REST API design, security (OAuth2/JWT), and Postman. • Familiarity with Event\\-Driven Architecture (RabbitMQ, Kafka). •
 **AI \\& Agentic Framework Exposure:**

title: AI/ML Engineer
description:
**\\#WeAreHiring** 




  





**Role: AI/ML Engineer** 

 (Voice AI • Conversational Systems • Execution \\& Optimisation)
 




**Location:** 
 Chennai (Hybrid)
 



**Company:** 
 Allsec AI
 



**Website:** 
 www.allsec.ai
 



**Engagement Type:** 
 Full\\-Time \\| Early\\-Stage Start\\-up
 



  





**About Allsec AI**




 Allsec AI is an
 **AI\\-native, digital\\-first, omni\\-channel CX managed services company** 
 . We help enterprises unlock scalable, efficient, and real\\-time customer experience operations through agentic AI, intelligent workflows, and automation. We are
 **redefining outsourcing** 
 by delivering packaged, outcome\\-led services, with
 **full ownership of delivery outcomes** 
 , not just technology.
 



  





**About This Role**




 The core engineering challenge of this role is to
 **create AI voice\\-based CX within a trifecta of cost, quality, and latency** 
 . You will execute on decisions that balance all three (across voice models, language models, and real\\-time pipelines) in a production CX environment where those trade\\-offs have direct business consequences.
 



  





 You will be working on a
 **live, multi\\-model AI platform where your responsibility lies in contributing meaningfully to making it better, faster, and more economical** 
 , under the technical direction of the Principal Engineer. You will be expected to ship, optimise, debug, and iterate, often across layers of the stack you may not have originally written.
 



  





 Beyond the core platform, you will support the Principal Engineer and operational leadership team in developing new use cases, building out conversation flows, and contributing to the product’s expansion into new verticals.
 



  





*If you are someone who thinks about AI not just as a technical system but* 
***as something that has to work for a real person***
*on the other end of a phone call, this role is built for you.* 




  





**Key Responsibilities**




**Voice AI \\& Model Optimisation**



* Work on and improve the end\\-to\\-end voice pipeline — STT, TTS, interruption handling, silence detection, and turn\\-taking logic.
* Execute on optimisations across the cost\\-quality\\-latency trifecta: implement caching, streaming, and model selection decisions under technical guidance.
* Improve STT accuracy across accents, call environments, and language variations through systematic testing and tuning.
* Evaluate new voice and language models and provide recommendations to the Principal Engineer on provider selection.



  





**LLM Integration \\& Pipeline Engineering**



* Build, maintain, and improve LLM pipelines including RAG, prompt engineering, and context management.
* Work across multiple LLM providers — benchmarking based on cost, latency, and output quality as directed.
* Contribute to evaluation frameworks that measure model performance across cost, quality, and latency dimensions.
* Manage and improve vector databases, embedding pipelines, and retrieval systems that power the knowledge layer.



  





**Use Case \\& Conversation Support**



* Support the translation of client requirements into conversation flows and call scripts, working alongside the operational and business team.
* Build and refine branching logic for multi\\-turn voice conversations — handling edge cases, fallbacks, and escalation paths.
* Contribute ideas and execution on new use cases as the business grows.
* Assist the Principal Engineer in extending existing flows into new industry verticals — from BFSI to healthcare, retail, and beyond.



  





**Platform \\& Infrastructure**



* Be operationally self\\-sufficient — deploy, monitor, and debug your own work across the existing multi\\-cloud stack (Digital Ocean, Azure, GCP).
* Contribute to basic UI/UX improvements where the AI layer meets the client\\-facing product.
* Participate in code reviews, maintain clean and documented code, and work within engineering standards set by the Principal Engineer.



  





**Requirements**



* 4\\+ years of software development experience — strong development fundamentals are non\\-negotiable; you must be able to write clean, production\\-quality code.
* Hands\\-on experience with LLM APIs and integration (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, or equivalent) in a live product environment — not just experimentation.
* Familiarity with RAG pipelines, vector databases (Pinecone, FAISS, or similar), and prompt engineering.
* Enough cloud and DevOps competence to own your own deployments — you do not need a separate DevOps resource to ship your work.
* A builder mindset — comfortable inheriting a codebase you did not write, figuring things out without a playbook, and delivering reliably under technical direction.
* Interest in the product and use case side of AI — not just the engineering.



  





**Nice to Have**



* Experience working on conversational AI, voice bots, or contact centre technology.
* Working experience with STT and/or TTS systems.
* Exposure to MLOps practices — model versioning, evaluation pipelines, and testing of model variants.
* Prior start\\-up or early\\-stage product experience where you have worked across multiple areas of the stack.
* Comfort working in a multi\\-stakeholder environment with both technical and non\\-technical collaborators.



  





**Why Join Allsec AI**



* Work on one of the hardest and
 **most interesting problems in applied AI** 
 : making voice AI that actually works, for real enterprise clients.
* Learn directly from a Principal Engineer with deep technical expertise, in an environment where good work is visible and recognised.
* The work you do will have
 **direct, visible impact on a product** 
 used by real enterprise clients.
* A front\\-row seat to
 **building an AI\\-native company from its earliest stage** 
 , with equity upside for the right candidate.
* **Competitive compensation** 
 commensurate with experience.



  





**How to Apply**




 Interested candidates, please send your resume to:
 **careers@allsec.ai, gukhan.allsec@outlook.com**

For the first time ever, you can manage and automate every part of the employee lifecycle in a single system.
   

  

 Take onboarding, for example. With Rippling, you can hire a new employee anywhere in the world and set up their payroll, corporate card, computer, benefits, and even third\\-party apps like Slack and Microsoft 365 \\- all within 90 seconds.
   

  

 Based in San Francisco, CA, Rippling has raised $1\\.85B\\+ from the world’s top investors \\- including Kleiner Perkins, Founders Fund, Sequoia, Greenoaks, Bedrock, GIC, Elad Gil and Goldman Sachs—and was named one of America's best startup employers by Forbes.
   

  

**About The Role**

title: AI Agent Development Engineer
description:
**Job Description**
**In this Role, Your Responsibilities Will Be:**
 In this role, you will help shape the future of intelligent engineering and power plant operations by developing hands\\-on AI/ML and agent\\-based solutions that simplify engineering work, increase productivity, and unlock new operational capabilities.
   

  

 You will build intelligent tools and AI agents that automate control logic generation, graphics configuration, reporting, and engineering workflows, enabling plant engineers to focus on higher\\-value innovation. By analyzing control system and operational data, you will uncover patterns, develop predictive capabilities, and deliver data\\-driven decision support for real\\-world power plant environments.
   

  

* Apply AI\\-based anomaly detection, diagnostics, and decision\\-support solutions to real power\\-plant operational challenges.
* Translate plant operating behavior, control logic, alarms, and historian trends into practical AI use cases and agent workflows.
* Work with time\\-series operational data from DCS/SCADA and plant historians (preferably Emerson Ovation) to identify abnormal patterns, degradation, and performance losses.
* Collaborate with sales and marketing,and reliability teams to validate AI insights against real plant conditions.
* Support development and tuning of AI/analytics models using Python and data tools (focus on application rather than algorithm research).
* Integrate AI outputs into dashboards, alerts, and engineering workflows without impacting plant safety or control integrity.
* Test, validate, and monitor AI solutions in live or near\\-real plant environments, ensuring reliability and low false\\-alarm rates.
* Continuously improve AI performance using operational feedback, historical fault data, and domain knowledge.
* Troubleshoot deployed analytics/AI tools and coordinate improvements with software or data teams.


**Who you Are:**
 We are looking for a proactive professional who excels in dynamic, fast\\-paced environments. You demonstrate exceptional initiative, confidently tackling complex challenges in power plant control and optimization. With a strong commitment to innovation and excellence, you set ambitious goals and uphold the highest standards in every project.
   

  

**For This Role, You Will Need:**
* Bachelor's degree in Electrical, Instrumentation, Mechanical, Control Systems, Power Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field.
* 2–8 years of experience in coal\\-fired, combined\\-cycle, or utility power plants with strong knowledge of plant operations, control processes, and performance optimization.

title: Principal Engineer
description:
The Principal Engineer is the most senior individual contributor in the System Platform Team. This is not an architect\\-in\\-an\\-ivory\\-tower role \\- it is a hands\\-on technical leadership position where roughly 60% of time is spent writing production code and 40% on cross\\-cutting technical responsibilities: architecture, technology evaluation, design reviews, and mentoring. The expectation is that when the team runs into its hardest technical problem, the Principal is the one who unblocks it \\- not by delegating, but by pairing or by writing the solution.
   

  

 Over the course of the year, the Principal intentionally rotates across different engineering domains \\- developer experience, release engineering, data and messaging, incident and compliance tooling. This breadth is deliberate: by the end of the year, the Principal has deep firsthand knowledge of the full platform codebase, which is what makes their architecture reviews, technology decisions, and mentorship credible across all areas.
   

  

**The Stack You Will Work On**
* A Java/Spring Boot microservices platform (GreyMatter) running on GKE, backed by Kafka, PostgreSQL, Redis, and MinIO/S3
* An Erlang/OTP real\\-time robot\\-control system (Butler Core) running on GCP VMs, using Mnesia, EMQX (MQTT broker), and Cowboy HTTP
* A hybrid CI/CD setup spanning GitHub (mono\\-repo for 19 platform services) and Bitbucket (legacy/firmware repositories), with Jenkins, Spinnaker, ArgoCD, Nexus, Terraform, and Ansible
* Kafka\\-based event streaming with Avro schema contracts and Redis for payload management
* An internal context\\-retrieval server that surfaces runbooks, tickets, deployment history, and architecture docs as LLM\\-queryable tools
* An incident management platform (evaluating commercial tools vs. in\\-house) with a custom compliance evidence layer for SOC2/ISO audits
* A home\\-grown Just\\-In\\-Time access portal covering GKE, GCP IAM, Kafka ACLs, SQL, Mnesia, and config management, integrated with Google Workspace SSO.


**Responsibilites**
**Architecture \\& Technical Direction**
* Lead cross\\-team architecture reviews \\- help the team make large technical decisions, document trade\\-offs, and land on durable solutions
* Own build\\-vs\\-buy decisions: evaluate commercial tools against in\\-house alternatives with documented analysis, and live with the consequences
* Define and enforce engineering standards across the whole team: schema conventions, observability patterns, security postures, secrets hygiene, API design
* Review system designs across every engineering area the team works in \\- not just the area you are currently contributing to
* Partner with the Associate Director on technical hiring: contribute to interview design, loop calibration, and leveling decisions


**Hands\\-On Engineering**
* Rotate across different engineering domains throughout the year, contributing meaningfully to each \\- shipping production code, not just reviewing it
* Write and maintain design documents and architecture decision records (ADRs) \\- these are required deliverables, not optional write\\-ups
* Unblock engineers on hard problems by pairing directly, not by delegating to someone else
* Build and own cross\\-cutting libraries, shared tooling, and foundational components that multiple services depend on
* Mentorship \\& Culture
* Technically mentor the Staff Engineers \\- paired design sessions, deep code review, escalation target for hard problems
* Raise the bar in code review: model the level of feedback and depth of analysis that sets the team standard
* Drive a blameless engineering culture \\- incidents and mistakes are learning opportunities, not blame events
* Treat documentation as first\\-class engineering output: design docs and runbooks ship with features, not after them


**Requirements**
* 14\\+ years of software engineering with at least 5 years at Staff Engineer level or equivalent senior IC level
* Has shipped at least one platform, developer\\-experience, or infrastructure product at scale that other engineering teams measurably adopted \\- not just delivered, but adopted and continued to use
* Production experience across at least four of: Kafka, Kubernetes, GitOps and ArgoCD, Grafana/InfluxDB observability stacks, OAuth2/OIDC identity systems, LLM orchestration or RAG systems
* Demonstrated build\\-vs\\-buy judgment: has made significant make\\-or\\-buy decisions, documented the trade\\-offs, and lived with the outcome
* Deep system design instincts \\- can hold a 10\\+ service distributed system in mind and identify coupling problems and failure modes before they become production incidents
* Excellent written communication \\- drives technical influence through documents, not just verbal authority in meetings
* Comfortable operating as the senior\\-most IC on a small team where pragmatism and real constraints shape decisions


**Nice to Have**
* Familiarity with Erlang/OTP, Mnesia, or BEAM VM tooling \\- for context on the Butler Core platformExperience with hybrid deployment environments spanning Kubernetes and traditional VMs in the same production system
* Has led a technical governance body \\- architecture review board, RFC process, or equivalent cross\\-team decision\\-making forum
* Background evaluating and procuring engineering tooling at a technical (not just commercial) level
* Familiarity with VDA5050 or AGV/robotics communication protocols

title: Founding AI Engineer
description:
At LTV.ai, we're redefining email marketing for e\\-commerce brands by automating a lot of the day\\-to\\-day manual grunt work that goes into email marketing, from segmentation, to creating campaigns without designers, to personalization.
   

  

 While increasing LTV and driving measurable growth, we help brands stay focused on what matters most \\- the customer.
   

  

 Why join us?
   

  

 We're a fast\\-moving team building a high\\-growth company that's transforming the e\\-commerce industry.
   

  

 As an early engineering hire, you'll own meaningful surface area from day one — shipping AI features, shaping architecture, and building the data infrastructure that powers the platform.
   

  

 You'll have the autonomy to drive features end\\-to\\-end and the leverage of unlimited AI tokens to move at a pace most teams can't match.
   

  

 Recent wins
   

  

* Went from 0 to $1M ARR in 6 months and just closed a growth round of funding
* Built a segmentation engine that personalizes interactions across 70m\\+ consumers per month (growing 10% MoM) using e\\-commerce platform data, in a compliant manner, at scale
* Stood up a data pipeline handling millions of events daily
* Built an agentic tool\\-calling chat flow that automates email campaign production at scale
* Built a chat\\-to\\-DB layer that proactively suggests customer lists to send to
* Enabled multi\\-LLM routing for a single interaction, optimized for performance while reducing costs


 We'd like to work on
   

  

* Deepening our AI infrastructure: customer\\-level memory, agentic workflows, smarter model routing, and self\\-optimizing conversation engines
* Scaling our data pipelines to handle the next order of magnitude of events without compromising latency or cost
* Expanding self\\-service so any e\\-commerce brand can onboard and get value from LTV.ai without white\\-glove handoff
* Pushing our image and content generation capabilities so brands can ship a wider range of emails at lower cost
* Driving proactivity across the platform — from recommending the right customer lists and campaigns to expanding beyond email into multi\\-channel


 Role Responsibilities
   

  

* Own features end\\-to\\-end — from architecture and data modeling through implementation, rollout, and iteration in production
* Work across the full backend surface: AI features, core platform architecture, and data pipelines that move millions of events daily
* Use AI coding tools aggressively (unlimited tokens) to ship faster — while taking full responsibility for the quality, correctness, and maintainability of every line that lands in main
* Design and build AI\\-native systems: multi\\-agent workflows, model routing, evals, and real\\-time feedback loops
* Make architectural calls that hold up under growth — and revisit them when they don't
* Pair closely with product, AI, and data on the highest\\-leverage work, not just the assigned tickets
* Raise the engineering bar: code review, design review, and pushing back when something isn't ready for production


 What we're looking for (ideally)
   

  

* 4\\+ years of backend engineering experience with a track record of owning systems end\\-to\\-end in production
* Strong engineering taste — you know the difference between shipped code and AI\\-generated slop, and you ship the former. Lines of code are not a flex; durable, well\\-reasoned systems are.
* AI\\-native mindset — you instinctively reach for AI tools to move faster, but you treat their output as a draft, not a deliverable. You can oversee AI\\-generated work and catch what it gets wrong.
* Experience scaling production systems in fast\\-moving SaaS, messaging, or e\\-commerce environments
* Comfortable with TypeScript (NestJS) and AWS\\-based infrastructure, or able to ramp quickly
* Real interest in LLMOps, AI application infrastructure, and the tooling around evals, prompt management, and agentic systems
* Pragmatic and adaptable — you can hold speed and quality at the same time and don't see them as a tradeoff
* Founder mentality — you build systems, you don't wait to inherit them


 Our Stack
   

  

* Frontend: React (TypeScript)
* Backend: NestJS (TypeScript)
* Infrastructure: AWS managed with Terraform
* Databases: PostgreSQL \\& ClickHouse


 Compensation \\+ Benefits
   

  

* Competitive compensation, equity, and a commitment to your personal and professional growth.


 If this role sounds like it could be a fit, we'd love to hear from you! Feel free to reach out to our founder directly \\- asad@ltv.ai.
   

  

 Not looking but know someone who would make a great fit? Refer them! We pay up to $5000 per

* Apply AIML system architecture and design patterns by selecting the blueprint that best fits use case needs. Contribute to AI Architecture by suggesting new patterns, identifying innovative approaches, and improving existing ones.

* Apply modern distributed system design patterns when suitable, including architectural sagas, Command Query Responsibility Segregation (CQRS), event\\-driven architectures, publish\\-subscribe models, and point\\-to\\-point messaging.
* Required Skills \\& Experience:

* Experience Range – 4 to 6 Years
* Professional experience in Machine Learning, Software Engineering, or a related field, with focused on designing and delivering AI/ML solutions in production environments.
* Hands\\-on experience with Generative AI and Agentic AI solutions, including RAG architectures, semantic search, embedding models, and representation and generative models such as BERT and GPT.
* Strong programming experience in Python, including at least 3\\+ years building production services using FastAPI, Asyncio, and Pydantic.
* Experience working with single\\- and multi\\-agent frameworks such as LangChain, LangGraph, or CrewAI, and familiarity with state\\-of\\-the\\-art commercial and open\\-source foundation models.
* Hands\\-on experience with cloud\\-based GenAI and AI platforms, including AWS SageMaker and Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, Vertex AI Search, and Vertex AI RAG Engine.
* Experience setting up and managing Jupyter environments, AutoML workflows, experimentation tracking, model serving, and monitoring on cloud\\-based MLOps platforms.
* Experience delivering production\\-grade APIs and microservices using modern software engineering practices.
* Hands\\-on experience building DevOps and CI/CD pipelines (e.g., Jenkins or similar), managing cloud deployments using infrastructure as code (Terraform), and collaborating through GitHub.
* Experience applying software engineering best practices and architectural patterns, including SOLID principles, 12\\-Factor App methodology, inversion of control (IoC), and sagas.
* Experience with identity and access management solutions, including OAuth 2\\.1 and OpenID Connect (OIDC).
* Hands\\-on experience with ML and AI frameworks and libraries such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, Keras, scikit\\-learn, Hugging Face, LangChain, NumPy, and Pandas.
* Experience in traditional machine learning, including feature engineering, exploratory data analysis (EDA), model training, and hyperparameter tuning using techniques such as XGBoost, GLMs, KNN, PCA, and SVM.
* Experience designing, building, and deploying end\\-to\\-end data, ML, and RAG pipelines.
* Experience working in lean, agile environments using Scaled Agile Framework (SAFe) or similar methodologies.
* Experience using DevSecOps tools such as Nexus, SonarQube, Checkmarx, and mcp\\-scan.
* Demonstrated ability to communicate complex technical concepts to both technical and non\\-technical audiences and influence leadership decisions.
